evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD emphasizes evolving and expanding usage of the data stack with no mention of testing, governance, or compliance signals, and this is the organization's first data hire framed around delivery.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role serves an esports arena business with no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language, placing errors squarely in the low-consequence, non-revenue-critical category.
evidence.data_team_maturity: as the organization's first dedicated data team member
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD explicitly states this is the first data hire tasked with building the function, the clearest possible early-stage signal.
evidence.jd_authorship: Maintaining and advancing data infrastructure through GitHub Actions and Pulumi
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Naming specific tools (BigQuery, dbt, Lightdash, GitHub Actions, Pulumi) in the responsibilities section with precise application context shows someone who has done this job wrote it.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Developing analytics use cases collaboratively with business teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is internal business teams consuming dashboards and analytics rather than a specific commercial, product, or finance function, and the role is building the data function itself.
evidence.autonomy_level: leadership capabilities for future team building
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: As the first data hire reporting directly to the COO with an eye toward building a team, the role is expected to define direction rather than execute scoped work.
evidence.ai_role: Paving the way for ML/IA applications
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build data infrastructure that will eventually support ML/AI applications, making them an enabler rather than a user of AI tools.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The JD contains no mention of testing, data contracts, or data quality frameworks anywhere in the text.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is framed entirely around building, integrating, and democratizing data capability with no risk, compliance, or reliability register present.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Managing the current data infrastructure and integrating new data sources
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: As the first data hire building out infrastructure and paving the way for new capabilities, this is a greenfield build despite some existing tooling in place.
